Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

Interface Configuration

All Known Subinterfaces:
GuavaRequirements, JavaRequirements
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultGuavaRequirements, DefaultJavaRequirements, MainConfiguration, Requirements
public interface Configuration
Configures the behavior of a single verifier.

Implementations must be thread-safe.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    boolean assertionsAreEnabled()
    Indicates if if assertThat() should delegate to requireThat(); otherwise, it won't do anything.
    Map<String,​Object> getContext()
    Returns an unmodifiable map to append to the exception message.
    boolean isCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates if exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
    boolean isDiffEnabled()
    Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    Configuration putContext​(String name, Object value)
    Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message.
    Configuration removeContext​(String name)
    Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
    String toString​(Object o)
    Returns the String representation of an object.
    Configuration withAssertionsDisabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
    Configuration withAssertionsEnabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
    Configuration withCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
    Configuration withConfiguration​(Configuration configuration)
    Replaces a verifier's configuration.
    Configuration withDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    Configuration withoutCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
    Configuration withoutDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    <T> Configuration withoutStringConverter​(Class<T> type)
    Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
    <T> Configuration withStringConverter​(Class<T> type, Function<T,​String> converter)
    Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

  • Method Details

    • assertionsAreEnabled

      boolean assertionsAreEnabled()
      Indicates if if assertThat() should delegate to requireThat(); otherwise, it won't do anything.
      Returns:
      true if assertions are enabled for this class

    • withAssertionsEnabled

      Configuration withAssertionsEnabled()
      Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withAssertionsDisabled

      Configuration withAssertionsDisabled()
      Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • isDiffEnabled

      boolean isDiffEnabled()
      Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      true by default

    • withDiff

      Configuration withDiff()
      Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withoutDiff

      Configuration withoutDiff()
      Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • isCleanStackTrace

      boolean isCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates if exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
      Returns:
      true by default
      See Also:
      withCleanStackTrace(), withoutCleanStackTrace()

    • withCleanStackTrace

      Configuration withCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      See Also:
      isCleanStackTrace()

    • withoutCleanStackTrace

      Configuration withoutCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      See Also:
      isCleanStackTrace()

    • getContext

      Map<String,​Object> getContext()
      Returns an unmodifiable map to append to the exception message.
      Returns:
      an empty map by default
      See Also:
      putContext(String, Object)

    • putContext

      Configuration putContext​(String name, Object value)
      Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message. Overrides any values associated with the name at the ThreadRequirements level.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      value - the value of the parameter
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null

    • removeContext

      Configuration removeContext​(String name)
      Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null

    • toString

      String toString​(Object o)
      Returns the String representation of an object. By default, custom handlers are provided for arrays, Integer, Long, BigDecimal, and Path.
      Parameters:
      o - an object
      Returns:
      the String representation of the object
      See Also:
      withStringConverter(Class, Function)

    • withStringConverter

      <T> Configuration withStringConverter​(Class<T> type, Function<T,​String> converter)
      Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

      Please note that type must be an exact match (e.g. configuring a converter for Set will not match values of type HashSet).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted (non-primitive arrays are mapped to Object[].class)
      converter - a function that converts an object of the specified type to a String
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any of the arguments are null

    • withoutStringConverter

      <T> Configuration withoutStringConverter​(Class<T> type)
      Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type is null

    • withConfiguration

      Configuration withConfiguration​(Configuration configuration)
      Replaces a verifier's configuration.
      Parameters:
      configuration - a new configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if configuration is null
